key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that adhere well to steel and still recover martensitic shape-memory behavior after annealing.
engineering_relevance: This enables functional NiTi surface coatings for wear, friction, corrosion, and thermal-actuation applications where conventional spray routes often fail.
method: NiTi powder was deposited on mild steel by high-velocity air-fuel (HVAF) thermal spraying.
Four processing parameter sets were compared to assess coating quality.
Coatings were then evaluated for composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 100–300 μm coatings that showed martensitic transformation and functional actuation in 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests after annealing.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are microstructurally inhomogeneous and defect-rich, so post-spray annealing is required to obtain useful shape-memory performance.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
